extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rohan won the 18.5km technical time trial by 5 seconds over CAVAGNA Rémi, with YATES Adam's unexpected 35-second deficit positioning INEOS perfectly for mountain stages.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan maximized time gains through technical descents and corners rather than pure power sections, beating CAVAGNA by 5 seconds.
YATES Adam lost only 7 seconds to ALMEIDA João in 18.5km TT, reversing UAE Tour pattern where he conceded 30+ seconds.
ALMEIDA João must defend jersey against PORTE Richie and YATES Adam who sit 6-7 seconds back with superior climbing credentials.
YATES Simon losing 42 seconds forces aggressive mountain racing, while brother YATES Adam rides defensively with GC position secured.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: First win since 2019 Worlds, cornering superiority over pure power specialists remains decisive advantage in technical TTs
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: 35-second deficit to ALMEIDA represents massive TT improvement from UAE Tour, now positioned 7 seconds behind with climbing advantage
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Consistent TT performance gaining 28 seconds on YATES Adam insufficient given 6-7 second gap to INEOS climbers behind
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Lost 5 seconds to DENNIS through technical sections despite pure power advantage, confirms cornering as limiting factor
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: 35-second deficit to DENNIS while sitting 6 seconds behind race lead shows form recovery post-Paris-Nice crash
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: 55-second TT loss creates tactical dilemma with KRUIJSWIJK Steven positioned higher on GC for team leadership
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: 42-second deficit forces high-risk attacking strategy against brother with superior position
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: 40-second TT loss demands mountain aggression without demonstrated ability to distance YATES Adam on climbs
